---
title: "NAF"
---

## The NAF 2025 nomenclature

The **Nomenclature of French Activities 2025 (NAF 2025)** is France's official classification of economic activities, aligned with **NACE Rev. 2.1**. Every business unit is assigned an **APE code** from this nomenclature, which underpins official economic statistics across Europe. It organises all activities into five nested hierarchical levels; a full interactive breakdown is available in the [appendix](#detailed-breakdown-of-the-naf-2025).

The training dataset was built by relabelling INSEE's previous 2.7-million-observation corpus from NACE 2.0 to NACE 2.1. While 75% of codes map one-to-one and were converted by direct table lookup, the remaining 25% are *multivocal* (one old code → several candidates) and, due to their overrepresentation in practice, account for ~52% of observations. These required reading the activity description to resolve. A **Rule-Based Augmented Generation (RBAG)** pipeline was developed for this: given a description and its legacy NACE 2.0 code, the correspondence table retrieves the 2–5 candidate NACE 2.1 codes, their official explanatory notes are injected into a prompt, and an LLM picks the best match. Evaluated on a 30,000-observation benchmark annotated by ~25 experts (strict inter-annotator agreement: 62%), an ensemble of three LLMs reaches **78% accuracy** — with much of the gap attributable to genuine ambiguity rather than model errors. The final corpus contains ~2.3 million NAF 2025 labels. More details in the [codif-ape-nace-revision](https://github.com/InseeFrLab/codif-ape-nace-revision/tree/prod) repository.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import HTML
HTML("""
<style>
.naf {
  max-width: 500px;
  font-family: -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
  margin: 1.6rem auto 2rem;
}
.naf-card {
  display: flex;
  border: 1px solid #dde3ec;
  border-radius: 8px;
  overflow: hidden;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: border-color .2s, box-shadow .2s;
  user-select: none;
}
.naf-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,.07); }
.naf-bar  { width: 5px; flex-shrink: 0; transition: width .2s; }
.naf-card.open .naf-bar { width: 7px; }
.naf-body { flex: 1; padding: 14px 20px; }
.naf-head {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: space-between;
  gap: 12px;
}
.naf-name { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; }
.naf-right { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 14px; }
.naf-count-wrap { text-align: right; line-height: 1; }
.naf-n    { font-size: 30px; font-weight: 700; opacity: .5; line-height: 1; }
.naf-n-lbl { font-size: 9px; color: #b0b8c8; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .06em; margin-top: 2px; }
.naf-chevron { font-size: 11px; color: #c0c8d8; transition: transform .25s; line-height: 1; }
.naf-card.open .naf-chevron { transform: rotate(180deg); }
.naf-fmt {
  display: inline-block;
  font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  font-weight: 600;
  padding: 2px 9px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  margin-top: 6px;
}
.naf-desc {
  font-size: 12px;
  color: #555;
  line-height: 1.55;
  max-height: 0;
  overflow: hidden;
  opacity: 0;
  transition: max-height .3s ease, opacity .25s ease, margin-top .3s ease;
  margin-top: 0;
}
.naf-card.open .naf-desc {
  max-height: 80px;
  opacity: 1;
  margin-top: 9px;
}
.naf-sep {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  padding: 2px 0;
}
.naf-sep-line  { width: 1px; height: 7px; background: #d4dae6; }
.naf-sep-stat  { font-size: 10px; color: #b5bfcf; padding: 2px 0; white-space: nowrap; }
.naf-sep-arrow { font-size: 12px; color: #cdd3de; line-height: 1; }
.naf-hint { font-size: 11px; color: #b0b8c8; margin-bottom: 8px; font-style: italic; text-align: center; }
</style>

<div class="naf">
<p style="text-align:center; font-size:16px; font-weight:700; color:#1a2a4a; margin-bottom:4px; letter-spacing:.01em;">NAF 2025 — Hierarchical structure</p>
<p class="naf-hint">Click on a level to display its description.</p>

<div class="naf-card" onclick="this.classList.toggle('open')" style="background:linear-gradient(to right,rgba(13,59,119,.07) 2.8%,#fafbff 2.8%)">
  <div class="naf-bar" style="background:#0D3B77"></div>
  <div class="naf-body">
    <div class="naf-head">
      <span class="naf-name" style="color:#0D3B77">Section</span>
      <div class="naf-right">
        <div class="naf-count-wrap">
          <div class="naf-n" style="color:#0D3B77">21</div>
          <div class="naf-n-lbl">codes</div>
        </div>
        <span class="naf-chevron">▼</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <span class="naf-fmt" style="color:#0D3B77; background:#e8edf8;">1 letter &nbsp;·&nbsp; A to U</span>
    <div class="naf-desc">21 broad thematic families (agriculture, industry, services…). Highest level of the nomenclature, standard granularity for macroeconomic analysis.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="naf-sep">
  <div class="naf-sep-line"></div>
  <span class="naf-sep-stat">≈ 4 divisions per section</span>
  <div class="naf-sep-arrow">▼</div>
</div>

<div class="naf-card" onclick="this.classList.toggle('open')" style="background:linear-gradient(to right,rgba(21,101,192,.07) 11.6%,#fafbff 11.6%)">
  <div class="naf-bar" style="background:#1565C0"></div>
  <div class="naf-body">
    <div class="naf-head">
      <span class="naf-name" style="color:#1565C0">Division</span>
      <div class="naf-right">
        <div class="naf-count-wrap">
          <div class="naf-n" style="color:#1565C0">87</div>
          <div class="naf-n-lbl">codes</div>
        </div>
        <span class="naf-chevron">▼</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <span class="naf-fmt" style="color:#1565C0; background:#e0e9f7;">2 digits &nbsp;·&nbsp; 01 to 99</span>
    <div class="naf-desc">First numeric level. Breaks each section into homogeneous activity domains; reference granularity for national and European sectoral statistics.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="naf-sep">
  <div class="naf-sep-line"></div>
  <span class="naf-sep-stat">≈ 3.3 groups per division</span>
  <div class="naf-sep-arrow">▼</div>
</div>

<div class="naf-card" onclick="this.classList.toggle('open')" style="background:linear-gradient(to right,rgba(25,118,210,.07) 38.4%,#fafbff 38.4%)">
  <div class="naf-bar" style="background:#1976D2"></div>
  <div class="naf-body">
    <div class="naf-head">
      <span class="naf-name" style="color:#1976D2">Group</span>
      <div class="naf-right">
        <div class="naf-count-wrap">
          <div class="naf-n" style="color:#1976D2">287</div>
          <div class="naf-n-lbl">codes</div>
        </div>
        <span class="naf-chevron">▼</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <span class="naf-fmt" style="color:#1976D2; background:#d8e6f6;">3 digits &nbsp;·&nbsp; XX.X</span>
    <div class="naf-desc">Refines the division into sets of activities sharing common economic characteristics. Intermediate level for detailed sectoral analysis.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="naf-sep">
  <div class="naf-sep-line"></div>
  <span class="naf-sep-stat">≈ 2.3 classes per group</span>
  <div class="naf-sep-arrow">▼</div>
</div>

<div class="naf-card" onclick="this.classList.toggle('open')" style="background:linear-gradient(to right,rgba(33,150,243,.07) 87.1%,#fafbff 87.1%)">
  <div class="naf-bar" style="background:#2196F3"></div>
  <div class="naf-body">
    <div class="naf-head">
      <span class="naf-name" style="color:#2196F3">Class</span>
      <div class="naf-right">
        <div class="naf-count-wrap">
          <div class="naf-n" style="color:#2196F3">651</div>
          <div class="naf-n-lbl">codes</div>
        </div>
        <span class="naf-chevron">▼</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <span class="naf-fmt" style="color:#2196F3; background:#cfe0fb;">4 digits &nbsp;·&nbsp; XX.XX</span>
    <div class="naf-desc">Detail level shared with the European NACE Rev. 2.1. Defines a specific activity; the level at which statistical harmonisation across EU member states takes place.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="naf-sep">
  <div class="naf-sep-line"></div>
  <span class="naf-sep-stat">≈ 1.1 sub-classes per class</span>
  <div class="naf-sep-arrow">▼</div>
</div>

<div class="naf-card" onclick="this.classList.toggle('open')" style="background:rgba(66,165,245,.07)">
  <div class="naf-bar" style="background:#42A5F5"></div>
  <div class="naf-body">
    <div class="naf-head">
      <span class="naf-name" style="color:#2b8fe0">Sub-class</span>
      <div class="naf-right">
        <div class="naf-count-wrap">
          <div class="naf-n" style="color:#2b8fe0">747</div>
          <div class="naf-n-lbl">codes</div>
        </div>
        <span class="naf-chevron">▼</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <span class="naf-fmt" style="color:#2b8fe0; background:#c5dafa;">4 digits + letter &nbsp;·&nbsp; XX.XXY</span>
    <div class="naf-desc">Finest level, specific to France. May split or merge classes to reflect national specificities. The trailing letter distinguishes sub-classes within the same class.</div>
  </div>
</div>

</div>
""")
```

## Multi-level classification

The five-level hierarchy of NAF 2025 opens a natural multi-task training strategy: rather than predicting only the finest level (sub-class), the model can be trained to simultaneously predict multiple levels of the hierarchy. Two theoretical benefits follow: (1) coarser levels are easier to classify and inject additional gradient signal that guides the shared representation towards features discriminant across the taxonomy; (2) at inference time the model directly outputs predictions at every level, requiring no post-hoc aggregation.

Three levels are retained, selected to cover the full granularity range of the nomenclature while remaining tractable: the **division** (first 2 characters, up to 84 codes in the training set), the **group** (first 4 characters, up to 586 codes), and the **sub-class** (full 5-character code, up to 680 codes). All three classification heads share a single **TokenEmbedder** — one WordPiece tokenizer and one embedding matrix — that is jointly optimised through the sum of three independent cross-entropy losses. Each level then has its own **SentenceEmbedder** (either mean pooling or label attention) and a **ClassificationHead** (a single linear projection). This design keeps the parameter count modest: only the three separate sentence embedders and linear heads add level-specific parameters on top of the shared trunk.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import HTML
HTML("""
<style>
.ml-outer {
  font-family: -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
  margin: 2rem 0;
}
.ml-card {
  border: 2px solid #d0d0d0;
  border-radius: 14px;
  background: #fff;
  padding: 28px 24px 20px;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 14px rgba(0,0,0,.06);
}
.ml-blk {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 9px;
  padding: 9px 12px;
  text-align: center;
}
.ml-icon { font-size: 17px; line-height: 1; }
.ml-name { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; margin-top: 3px; }
.ml-sub  { font-size: 8.5px; margin-top: 2px; }
.ml-arr {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  padding: 0 4px;
  gap: 1px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
/* hoverable blocks — same as .ah-block in index.qmd */
.ml-tip-target {
  cursor: default;
  transition: box-shadow .15s, transform .15s;
}
.ml-tip-target:hover {
  box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,.18);
  transform: translateY(-2px);
}
.ml-ashape { font-size: 8px;  font-weight: 600; color: #888; white-space: nowrap; }
.ml-aline  { font-size: 11px; color: #ccc; }
</style>

<div class="ml-outer">

<p style="text-align:center;font-size:14.5px;font-weight:700;color:#1a2a4a;margin:0 0 20px;letter-spacing:.01em;">
  Multi-level NACE Classification &mdash; Architecture
</p>

<div class="ml-card">

  <!-- INPUT -->
  <div style="display:flex;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:6px;">
    <div style="background:#f8f9fa;border:1.5px solid #ccc;border-radius:8px;padding:8px 24px;text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#999;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.07em;margin-bottom:3px;">Input</div>
      <div style="font-size:12px;color:#333;font-style:italic;">"Conseil en stratégie et management"</div>
      <div style="font-size:8.5px;color:#bbb;margin-top:2px;">(B,)&nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;str</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- arrow input → trunk -->
  <div style="display:flex;justify-content:center;height:18px;">
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;">
      <div style="width:1.5px;flex:1;background:#ccc;"></div>
      <div style="width:0;height:0;border-left:4px solid transparent;border-right:4px solid transparent;border-top:7px solid #ccc;"></div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- SHARED TRUNK -->
  <div style="background:#f3f5fa;border:1.5px solid #90a4ae;border-radius:10px;padding:14px 18px;">
    <div class="ml-tip-target" style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#78909c;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.1em;text-align:center;margin-bottom:10px;cursor:pointer;"
      data-tip="<b>Shared Token Encoder</b><br>WordPiece + Embedding + RMSNorm are computed <i>once</i> per input and reused by all N classification heads. This is the key multi-task design choice: all levels share the same token-level representations.<br><br>During backpropagation, gradients from all N losses flow through these shared parameters simultaneously — providing a richer learning signal than single-task training, and forcing the encoder to learn features useful at every level of the hierarchy.">
      Shared Token Encoder &mdash; weights shared across all levels
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;flex-wrap:nowrap;gap:0;">

      <!-- WordPiece -->
      <div class="ml-blk" style="background:#eceff1;border:1.5px solid #78909c;min-width:78px;">
        <div class="ml-icon" style="color:#546e7a;">&#9000;</div>
        <div class="ml-name" style="color:#37474f;">WordPiece</div>
        <div class="ml-sub"  style="color:#78909c;">vocab_size</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ml-arr">
        <div class="ml-ashape">(B, L)&nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;int64</div>
        <div class="ml-aline">&#9472;&#9472;&#9472;&#9472;&#9658;</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Embedding -->
      <div class="ml-blk" style="background:#e3f2fd;border:1.5px solid #1976d2;min-width:90px;">
        <div class="ml-icon" style="color:#1565c0;">&#8862;</div>
        <div class="ml-name" style="color:#0d47a1;">Embedding</div>
        <div class="ml-sub"  style="color:#1976d2;">V &times; d</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ml-arr">
        <div class="ml-ashape">(B, L, d)&nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;float32</div>
        <div class="ml-aline">&#9472;&#9472;&#9472;&#9472;&#9658;</div>
      </div>

      <!-- RMSNorm -->
      <div class="ml-blk" style="background:#e0f7fa;border:1.5px solid #0097a7;min-width:56px;">
        <div style="font-size:14px;font-weight:700;color:#006064;">&sigma;</div>
        <div class="ml-name" style="color:#00838f;">RMSNorm</div>
      </div>
      <div class="ml-arr">
        <div class="ml-ashape">(B, L, d)&nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;float32</div>
        <div class="ml-aline">&#9472;&#9472;&#9472;&#9472;&#9658;</div>
      </div>

      <!-- Token embeddings output label -->
      <div class="ml-blk" style="background:#ede7f6;border:1.5px solid #7b1fa2;min-width:100px;">
        <div style="font-size:13px;font-weight:700;color:#6a1b9a;">T</div>
        <div class="ml-name" style="color:#4a148c;">Token embeddings</div>
        <div class="ml-sub" style="color:#7b1fa2;">reused by every head</div>
      </div>

    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- FORK: trunk → 3 drops -->
  <div style="position:relative;height:50px;">
    <div style="position:absolute;top:0;left:50%;width:1.5px;height:26px;background:#bbb;transform:translateX(-50%);"></div>
    <div style="position:absolute;top:26px;left:12%;right:12%;height:1.5px;background:#bbb;"></div>
    <!-- left drop (Division) -->
    <div style="position:absolute;top:26px;left:12%;width:1.5px;height:22px;background:#1565C0;opacity:.6;transform:translateX(-50%);"></div>
    <div style="position:absolute;bottom:0;left:12%;transform:translateX(-50%);width:0;height:0;
                border-left:4px solid transparent;border-right:4px solid transparent;border-top:7px solid #1565C0;opacity:.6;"></div>
    <!-- centre drop (ghost) -->
    <div style="position:absolute;top:26px;left:50%;width:1.5px;height:22px;background:#aaa;opacity:.4;transform:translateX(-50%);"></div>
    <div style="position:absolute;bottom:0;left:50%;transform:translateX(-50%);width:0;height:0;
                border-left:3px solid transparent;border-right:3px solid transparent;border-top:6px solid #aaa;opacity:.4;"></div>
    <!-- right drop (Sub-class) -->
    <div style="position:absolute;top:26px;right:12%;width:1.5px;height:22px;background:#1e88e5;opacity:.6;transform:translateX(50%);"></div>
    <div style="position:absolute;bottom:0;right:12%;transform:translateX(50%);width:0;height:0;
                border-left:4px solid transparent;border-right:4px solid transparent;border-top:7px solid #1e88e5;opacity:.6;"></div>
  </div>

  <!-- Division | ··· | ghost Level k | ··· | Sub-class -->
  <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:5fr auto 4fr auto 5fr;gap:0;align-items:stretch;">

    <!-- ── DIVISION (Level 1) ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;gap:7px;">
      <div class="ml-tip-target" style="background:#e8edf8;border:1.5px solid #1565C0;border-radius:20px;padding:3px 10px;
                  font-size:8.5px;font-weight:700;color:#1565C0;letter-spacing:.05em;white-space:nowrap;"
        data-tip="<b>Level 1 — Division</b> (2-char prefix, e.g. &quot;70&quot;)<br>The coarsest level modelled. With only 84 classes it is the easiest to classify, providing a strong auxiliary gradient signal that anchors the shared representation to broad activity categories early in training.">
        Level 1 &middot; Division
      </div>
      <div class="ml-tip-target" style="width:100%;background:#e8edf8;border:1.5px solid #1565C0;border-radius:9px;padding:9px 6px;text-align:center;"
        data-tip="<b>Level-specific SentenceEmbedder</b><br>Each level has its own SentenceEmbedder with <i>independent</i> weights. The token embeddings (B, L, d) from the shared trunk are aggregated into a sentence-level representation in two possible ways:<br>• <b>Mean Pool</b> → (B, d): masked average over real tokens<br>• <b>Label Attention</b> → (B, C<sub>k</sub>, d): one representation per class via cross-attention<br>Having separate aggregators lets each level focus on different aspects of the token sequence.">
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#1565C0;">&#x2211;&divide;n</div>
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#0d47a1;">SentenceEmbedder</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#1976d2;margin-top:2px;">Mean Pool or Label Attn</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#888;margin-top:1px;">(B, d) or (B, C, d)</div>
      </div>
      <div style="width:1.5px;height:10px;background:#1565C0;opacity:.4;"></div>
      <div class="ml-tip-target" style="width:100%;background:#fff3e0;border:1.5px solid #f57c00;border-radius:9px;padding:9px 6px;text-align:center;"
        data-tip="<b>Level-specific Linear head</b><br>W<sub>1</sub> ∈ ℝ<sup>84 × d</sup> — the only level-specific parameters beyond the SentenceEmbedder. Maps the aggregated representation to 84 logits (one per Division code). Not shared with other levels.">
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#e65100;">&#x22B8;</div>
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#bf360c;">Linear</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#f57c00;">d &rarr; 84</div>
      </div>
      <div style="width:1.5px;height:10px;background:#bbb;"></div>
      <div style="width:100%;background:#f8f9fa;border:1.5px solid #90a4ae;border-radius:9px;padding:7px 6px;text-align:center;">
        <div style="font-size:11.5px;font-weight:700;color:#333;">&#375;&#770;<sub>1</sub></div>
        <div style="font-size:8px;color:#555;font-family:monospace;margin-top:2px;">"70"</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#888;margin-top:1px;">84 classes</div>
      </div>
      <div style="font-size:11px;color:#c62828;font-weight:700;">&Lscr;<sub>1</sub></div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── SEPARATOR LEFT ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:0 10px;">
      <span style="font-size:15px;color:#777;letter-spacing:4px;white-space:nowrap;">· · ·</span>
    </div>

    <!-- ── GHOST Level k ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;gap:7px;opacity:.82;">
      <div class="ml-tip-target" style="background:#f0f1f5;border:1.5px dashed #b0b8cc;border-radius:20px;padding:3px 10px;
                  font-size:8.5px;font-weight:700;color:#8890a8;letter-spacing:.05em;white-space:nowrap;"
        data-tip="<b>Any intermediate level k</b><br>The same three-block pattern (SentenceEmbedder → Linear → logits) repeats identically for every level between Level 1 and Level N. In our NAF setup: k = 2 corresponds to Group (4-char prefix, e.g. &quot;7010&quot;, 586 classes). Each level adds only its own SentenceEmbedder + Linear head on top of the shared trunk.">
        Level k &middot; &hellip;
      </div>
      <div style="width:100%;background:#f0f2f7;border:1.5px dashed #b8bece;border-radius:9px;padding:9px 6px;text-align:center;">
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#a0a8be;">&#x2211;&divide;n</div>
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#8890a8;">SentenceEmbedder</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#aab0c0;margin-top:2px;">Mean Pool or Label Attn</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#bbbfca;margin-top:1px;">(B, d) or (B, C, d)</div>
      </div>
      <div style="width:1.5px;height:10px;background:#c0c8d8;"></div>
      <div style="width:100%;background:#f5f3ee;border:1.5px dashed #ccc4b0;border-radius:9px;padding:9px 6px;text-align:center;">
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#c0b898;">&#x22B8;</div>
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#a8a090;">Linear</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#bcb49a;">d &rarr; C<sub>k</sub></div>
      </div>
      <div style="width:1.5px;height:10px;background:#c8c8c8;"></div>
      <div style="width:100%;background:#f8f8f8;border:1.5px dashed #c0c4cc;border-radius:9px;padding:7px 6px;text-align:center;">
        <div style="font-size:11.5px;font-weight:700;color:#9098a8;">&#375;&#770;<sub>k</sub></div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#a8aab8;margin-top:2px;">C<sub>k</sub> classes</div>
      </div>
      <div style="font-size:11px;color:#b06060;font-weight:700;">&Lscr;<sub>k</sub></div>
    </div>

    <!-- ── SEPARATOR RIGHT ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:0 10px;">
      <span style="font-size:15px;color:#777;letter-spacing:4px;white-space:nowrap;">· · ·</span>
    </div>

    <!-- ── SUB-CLASS (Level N) ── -->
    <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;gap:7px;">
      <div class="ml-tip-target" style="background:#dce8f5;border:1.5px solid #1e88e5;border-radius:20px;padding:3px 10px;
                  font-size:8.5px;font-weight:700;color:#1565c0;letter-spacing:.05em;white-space:nowrap;"
        data-tip="<b>Level N — Sub-class</b> (5-char code, e.g. &quot;7010Y&quot;)<br>The finest level and the primary classification target. 680 classes in the training split (out of 747 in the full NAF 2025). Benefits from the auxiliary gradient signal of all coarser levels, which pre-shapes the shared representation before the finest-level head has to distinguish between similar sub-classes.">
        Level N &middot; Sub-class
      </div>
      <div style="width:100%;background:#dce8f5;border:1.5px solid #1e88e5;border-radius:9px;padding:9px 6px;text-align:center;">
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#1e88e5;">&#x2211;&divide;n</div>
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#0d47a1;">SentenceEmbedder</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#1976d2;margin-top:2px;">Mean Pool or Label Attn</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#888;margin-top:1px;">(B, d) or (B, C, d)</div>
      </div>
      <div style="width:1.5px;height:10px;background:#1e88e5;opacity:.4;"></div>
      <div class="ml-tip-target" style="width:100%;background:#fff3e0;border:1.5px solid #f57c00;border-radius:9px;padding:9px 6px;text-align:center;"
        data-tip="<b>Level-specific Linear head</b><br>W<sub>N</sub> ∈ ℝ<sup>680 × d</sup> — maps the aggregated representation to 680 logits (one per Sub-class code). Entirely independent from the heads of other levels.">
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#e65100;">&#x22B8;</div>
        <div style="font-size:10px;font-weight:700;color:#bf360c;">Linear</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#f57c00;">d &rarr; 680</div>
      </div>
      <div style="width:1.5px;height:10px;background:#bbb;"></div>
      <div style="width:100%;background:#f8f9fa;border:1.5px solid #90a4ae;border-radius:9px;padding:7px 6px;text-align:center;">
        <div style="font-size:11.5px;font-weight:700;color:#333;">&#375;&#770;<sub>N</sub></div>
        <div style="font-size:8px;color:#555;font-family:monospace;margin-top:2px;">"7010Y"</div>
        <div style="font-size:7.5px;color:#888;margin-top:1px;">680 classes</div>
      </div>
      <div style="font-size:11px;color:#c62828;font-weight:700;">&Lscr;<sub>N</sub></div>
    </div>

  </div><!-- end grid -->

  <!-- JOINT LOSS -->
  <div style="margin-top:16px;display:flex;justify-content:center;">
    <div class="ml-tip-target" style="background:#fce4ec;border:1.5px solid #e91e63;border-radius:9px;padding:10px 28px;text-align:center;max-width:480px;"
      data-tip="<b>Joint multi-task loss</b><br>ℒ = Σ<sub>k</sub> CrossEntropy(ŷ<sub>k</sub>, y<sub>k</sub>)<br><br>Each level contributes an independent gradient signal. Coarser levels (Division, Group) are easier to classify and act as <i>auxiliary tasks</i> that regularise the shared token encoder — the model learns representations that are simultaneously discriminant at every granularity of the hierarchy.">
      <div style="font-size:9px;font-weight:700;color:#880e4f;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.08em;margin-bottom:5px;">
        Joint training loss
      </div>
      <div style="font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:#c2185b;">
        &Lscr; = &Lscr;<sub>div</sub> + &Lscr;<sub>grp</sub> + &Lscr;<sub>sub</sub>
      </div>
      <div style="font-size:8.5px;color:#ad1457;margin-top:4px;">
        Independent cross-entropy at each level &mdash; summed and back-propagated through the shared trunk
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

</div><!-- end ml-card -->

<!-- Tooltip box — same pattern as index.qmd #arch-tooltip-h -->
<div id="ml-tooltip-h"
  style="margin-top:.8rem;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;padding:12px 18px;
         background:#fff;border:2px solid #ddd;border-radius:6px;
         font-size:14px;color:#444;line-height:1.6;
         max-width:640px;display:none;text-align:center;
         transition:background .2s ease,border-color .2s ease;">
</div>

<p style="font-size:10px;color:#aaa;text-align:center;margin-top:8px;">
  Class counts (84 / 586 / 680) reflect codes observed in the training split (train_fraction = 0.13 of the full corpus).
</p>

</div><!-- end ml-outer -->

<script>
(function(){
  var tip = document.getElementById('ml-tooltip-h');
  document.querySelectorAll('.ml-tip-target').forEach(function(el){
    el.addEventListener('mouseenter', function(){
      tip.innerHTML = el.getAttribute('data-tip');
      tip.style.display = 'block';
      tip.style.background  = el.style.background || el.style.backgroundColor || '#fff';
      tip.style.borderColor = el.style.borderColor || '#ddd';
    });
    el.addEventListener('mouseleave', function(){
      tip.style.display = 'none';
    });
  });
})();
</script>
""")
```

## Detailed breakdown of the NAF 2025 {.appendix}

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
from IPython.display import HTML

with open('assets/naf2025_structure.json') as f:
    _naf = json.load(f)

_json = json.dumps(_naf, ensure_ascii=False)

_styles = """
<style>
.nafx {
  font-family: -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
  border: 1px solid #dde3ec;
  border-radius: 8px;
  overflow: hidden;
  margin: 1rem 0 2rem;
}
.nafx-bc {
  padding: 9px 16px;
  background: #f7f8fb;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e8edf5;
  font-size: 11px;
  color: #888;
  min-height: 34px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 6px;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}
.nafx-bc-item { font-weight: 600; }
.nafx-bc-sep  { color: #ccc; }
.nafx-cols {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 22fr 22fr 12fr 12fr 22fr;
}
.nafx-col { display: flex; flex-direction: column; }
.nafx-col + .nafx-col { border-left: 1px solid #eaeef5; }
.nafx-col-hdr {
  padding: 7px 12px;
  background: #f3f5fa;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e4e9f2;
  font-size: 10px;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: .07em;
  color: #778;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: space-between;
  white-space: nowrap;
}
.nafx-col-n {
  font-size: 10px;
  font-weight: 600;
  background: #e8edf5;
  color: #556;
  border-radius: 10px;
  padding: 1px 7px;
}
.nafx-body {
  overflow-y: auto;
  max-height: 280px;
  flex: 1;
}
.nafx-item {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 8px;
  padding: 7px 12px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #f3f5fa;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background .12s;
  font-size: 12px;
  color: #333;
}
.nafx-item:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
.nafx-item:hover { background: #f5f7fc; }
.nafx-item.active { background: #eef2fb; font-weight: 600; }
.nafx-strip {
  width: 3px;
  align-self: stretch;
  border-radius: 2px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
  min-height: 16px;
}
.nafx-code {
  font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
  font-size: 11px;
  font-weight: 700;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.nafx-lbl  { color: #555; line-height: 1.3; }
.nafx-empty {
  padding: 18px 12px;
  font-size: 11px;
  color: #bbb;
  font-style: italic;
}
.nafx-hint {
  font-size: 11px;
  color: #aaa;
  font-style: italic;
  padding: 10px 16px;
  border-top: 1px solid #eaeef5;
  text-align: center;
}
</style>
"""

_html = """
<div class="nafx">
  <div class="nafx-bc" id="nafx-bc">Select a section to explore the hierarchy.</div>
  <div class="nafx-cols">
    <div class="nafx-col">
      <div class="nafx-col-hdr">Section <span class="nafx-col-n">21</span></div>
      <div class="nafx-body" id="nafx-list-s"></div>
    </div>
    <div class="nafx-col">
      <div class="nafx-col-hdr">Division <span class="nafx-col-n" id="nafx-n-d">—</span></div>
      <div class="nafx-body" id="nafx-list-d"><div class="nafx-empty">← Select a section</div></div>
    </div>
    <div class="nafx-col">
      <div class="nafx-col-hdr">Group <span class="nafx-col-n" id="nafx-n-g">—</span></div>
      <div class="nafx-body" id="nafx-list-g"><div class="nafx-empty">← Select a division</div></div>
    </div>
    <div class="nafx-col">
      <div class="nafx-col-hdr">Class <span class="nafx-col-n" id="nafx-n-c">—</span></div>
      <div class="nafx-body" id="nafx-list-c"><div class="nafx-empty">← Select a group</div></div>
    </div>
    <div class="nafx-col">
      <div class="nafx-col-hdr">Sub-class <span class="nafx-col-n" id="nafx-n-sc">—</span></div>
      <div class="nafx-body" id="nafx-list-sc"><div class="nafx-empty">← Select a class</div></div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="nafx-hint">Groups and classes are identified by their code only (labels not available at these levels).</div>
</div>
"""

_script = r"""
<script>
(function() {
  const NAF = JSON.parse(document.getElementById('_naf_data').textContent);

  let selS = null, selD = null, selG = null, selC = null;

  function color() { return selS ? NAF.sections.find(s => s.id === selS).color : '#888'; }

  function item(code, label, active) {
    const c = color();
    const txt = label
      ? `<span class="nafx-code" style="color:${c}">${code}</span><span class="nafx-lbl">${label}</span>`
      : `<span class="nafx-code" style="color:${c}">${code}</span>`;
    return `<div class="nafx-item${active?' active':''}" data-id="${code}">
      <div class="nafx-strip" style="background:${c}"></div>${txt}</div>`;
  }

  function set(id, html) { document.getElementById(id).innerHTML = html || '<div class="nafx-empty">—</div>'; }
  function setN(id, n)   { document.getElementById(id).textContent = n; }

  function bc() {
    const parts = [];
    if (selS) { const s = NAF.sections.find(x => x.id===selS); parts.push(`<span class="nafx-bc-item" style="color:${s.color}">${s.id} — ${s.label}</span>`); }
    if (selD) { parts.push(`<span class="nafx-bc-sep">›</span><span class="nafx-bc-item">${selD} — ${NAF.divisions[selD].label}</span>`); }
    if (selG) { parts.push(`<span class="nafx-bc-sep">›</span><span class="nafx-bc-item">${selG}</span>`); }
    if (selC) { parts.push(`<span class="nafx-bc-sep">›</span><span class="nafx-bc-item">${selC}</span>`); }
    document.getElementById('nafx-bc').innerHTML = parts.length ? parts.join('') : 'Select a section to explore the hierarchy.';
  }

  function renderS() {
    set('nafx-list-s', NAF.sections.map(s =>
      `<div class="nafx-item${selS===s.id?' active':''}" data-id="${s.id}">
        <div class="nafx-strip" style="background:${s.color}"></div>
        <span class="nafx-code" style="color:${s.color}">${s.id}</span>
        <span class="nafx-lbl">${s.label}</span>
      </div>`).join(''));
    document.querySelectorAll('#nafx-list-s .nafx-item').forEach(el =>
      el.addEventListener('click', () => {
        selS = el.dataset.id; selD = null; selG = null; selC = null;
        renderS(); renderD(); clearG(); clearC(); clearSC(); bc();
      }));
  }

  function renderD() {
    if (!selS) { set('nafx-list-d','<div class="nafx-empty">← Select a section</div>'); setN('nafx-n-d','—'); return; }
    const divIds = NAF.sections.find(s => s.id===selS).divisions;
    set('nafx-list-d', divIds.map(id => item(id, NAF.divisions[id]?.label, selD===id)).join(''));
    setN('nafx-n-d', divIds.length);
    document.querySelectorAll('#nafx-list-d .nafx-item').forEach(el =>
      el.addEventListener('click', () => {
        selD = el.dataset.id; selG = null; selC = null;
        renderD(); renderG(); clearC(); clearSC(); bc();
      }));
  }

  function renderG() {
    if (!selD) { set('nafx-list-g','<div class="nafx-empty">← Select a division</div>'); setN('nafx-n-g','—'); return; }
    const grpIds = NAF.divisions[selD].groupes;
    set('nafx-list-g', grpIds.map(id => item(id, null, selG===id)).join(''));
    setN('nafx-n-g', grpIds.length);
    document.querySelectorAll('#nafx-list-g .nafx-item').forEach(el =>
      el.addEventListener('click', () => {
        selG = el.dataset.id; selC = null;
        renderG(); renderC(); clearSC(); bc();
      }));
  }

  function renderC() {
    if (!selG) { set('nafx-list-c','<div class="nafx-empty">← Select a group</div>'); setN('nafx-n-c','—'); return; }
    const clsIds = NAF.groupes[selG].classes;
    set('nafx-list-c', clsIds.map(id => item(id, null, selC===id)).join(''));
    setN('nafx-n-c', clsIds.length);
    document.querySelectorAll('#nafx-list-c .nafx-item').forEach(el =>
      el.addEventListener('click', () => {
        selC = el.dataset.id;
        renderC(); renderSC(); bc();
      }));
  }

  function renderSC() {
    if (!selC) { set('nafx-list-sc','<div class="nafx-empty">← Select a class</div>'); setN('nafx-n-sc','—'); return; }
    const scIds = NAF.classes[selC].sousclasses;
    set('nafx-list-sc', scIds.map(id => item(id, NAF.sousclasses[id], false)).join(''));
    setN('nafx-n-sc', scIds.length);
  }

  function clearG()  { selG=null; set('nafx-list-g','<div class="nafx-empty">← Select a division</div>'); setN('nafx-n-g','—'); }
  function clearC()  { selC=null; set('nafx-list-c','<div class="nafx-empty">← Select a group</div>');    setN('nafx-n-c','—'); }
  function clearSC() { set('nafx-list-sc','<div class="nafx-empty">← Select a class</div>');               setN('nafx-n-sc','—'); }

  renderS();
})();
</script>
"""

HTML(
    f'<script type="application/json" id="_naf_data">{_json}</script>\n'
    + _styles + _html + _script
)
```
